scope.setting: finite fuzzy-sphere/static-patch modes with axiomatic selection of positive-definite energy-difference Schur channels
scope.result_type: finite axiomatic regulator selection plus bounded bridge-diagnostic verification
scope.bounded_family: 1 <= L <= 5, four selected regulators, perturbation radius 0.05
scope.claim_boundary: finite axiomatic regulator selection only; not continuum de Sitter quantum gravity, dS/CFT, or literal ER=EPR in de Sitter

core_claim: Independent static-patch-looking axioms select the implemented Goal 27 regulator class without using bridge/response data as axioms; the selected class preserves the screen-shadow no-go and M_N versus C^N bridge distinction.

exact_finite_theorem_style_claims.claim: The finite selection axioms choose the Goal 27 accepted regulator class from the candidate primitive catalog.
No selected axiom mentions bridge algebra, M_N, C^N, off-diagonal response, or response gaps; a response-oracle candidate is rejected.
A positive-definite energy-difference Schur kernel with unit diagonal defines a CP, trace-preserving, unital finite observer channel preserving diagonal screen data.
Instant total dephasing satisfies the other channel/covariance requirements but fails vanishing cutoff-continuity, identifying that axiom as necessary.
For the selected bounded family, screen shadows collide while derived off-diagonal retention separates M_N from C^N.

obstruction_table.lesson: Raw heat transfer must be normalized or completed before it can be a screen-shadow-preserving observer channel.
Screen-only data do not supply the observer-channel completion needed for algebraic diagnostics.
CP/TP/unital static-patch covariance alone is insufficient; continuity to the identity in the cutoff limit is needed.
Selection cannot depend on the bridge/response diagnostic it is supposed to derive.

bounded_certificate_evidence.claim: Check cutoffs 1 through 5 for all selected regulators and perturbation variants.
Run focused tests for axiomatic selection, anti-tautology, missing-continuity counterexample, selected-class equality, and bridge diagnostics.

harlow_facing_summary: Goal 28 replaces the declared Goal 27 regulator class by a finite axiomatic selection rule. Static-patch covariance, positive-definite time averages, screen-preserving CPTP/unitality, KMS/heat balance, finite dilation, and vanishing cutoff continuity select the class; total dephasing shows why the continuity axiom is necessary.
limitations: The result is finite and axiomatic. It does not derive the axioms from continuum de Sitter gravity, a path integral, observer modular theory, or dS/CFT.
